title: cynanchum arenarium
intro: Cynanchum arenarium, also known as Sand-vetch or Carline Thistle, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and roots are used to make herbal preparations that are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. Cynanchum arenarium preparations are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or used topically to reap its medicinal properties.
